# Corrections changelog — Phase I internal cleanup

*Generated by `data/clean/build_clean_dataset.py` from the frozen `crawl/pages/*.json`. Baseline inputs unchanged. Deterministic.*

## Load & scope
Loaded 1589 pages across 14 units (1173 current-students, 300 study, 116 alumni).

law: collapsed 3 URL twins (3 canonical + 0 digit).
feit: collapsed 14 URL twins (13 canonical + 1 digit).
arts: collapsed 2 URL twins (2 canonical + 0 digit).
ffam: collapsed 1 URL twins (1 canonical + 0 digit).
TOTAL collapsed: 20. 1589 -> 1569 canonical pages.

## D1 — page_type classification
Current-students page_type split: content=843, archive=140, profile=96, stub=69, redirect=5

## D5 — chrome/binary flags
Binary-asset URLs flagged (excluded from word stats): 34 (33 on study).
Chrome hosts excluded from link aggregation: www.unimelb, about.unimelb, safety.unimelb, facebook, linkedin, instagram.

## D2 + D12 + full re-tag — topic count reconciliation (current-students)

`old` = pages carrying the tag under the legacy tags; `new(all)` = under corrected rules, any page_type; `new(content)` = corrected rules, content pages only (the canonical cleaned count). `fp` = 1 − new(content)/old.

| topic | old | new(all) | new(content) | fp_rate | note |

| subjects-timetable | 320 | 319 | 174 | 0.46 |  |
| orientation | 276 | 76 | 76 | 0.72 | D11 + tightened regex — FEIT inflation is partly content-framing, not only path nesting (see reconciliation) |
| research-candidature | 240 | 192 | 96 | 0.6 | D12 — absorbs graduate-research/HDR signals; Law GRD bios now page_type=profile (excluded) |
| IT-systems | 226 | 18 | 14 | 0.94 | D2 regex tightened (dropped bare \bIT\b/systems/portal; kept computing/computer-space) |

## Reconciliation note (full re-tag drift)

Per the agreed **full corpus re-tag**, every page was re-tagged from scratch with the corrected rules, so counts move for two reasons: (a) the named fixes (IT-systems, graduation, FEIT de-nest, page_type exclusions), and (b) drift where the unified ruleset differs from the unknown legacy processor that tagged the 11 legacy units. Rows above with no note and a non-zero fp_rate are drift, not a defect — they reflect one consistent ruleset replacing per-unit legacy tagging. The `new(content)` column is the canonical count.

**Phase II refinement (weighted scoring).** A tag now qualifies only if it matches the title/path (strong signal) or appears ≥2× in the excerpt; a single tangential excerpt mention no longer adds a tag. This removed the placements-WIL (+126→+27) and academic-skills (+53→+11) over-tagging surfaced in the first pass, plus the bare `learning`/`writing`/`maths` tokens in academic-skills. Otherwise-untagged pages keep their single strongest tag as a fallback.

### Drift flags — unified ruleset BROADER than legacy (review before quoting)

Topics where the corrected ruleset tags MORE pages than the legacy tags (negative fp_rate). These are not named D-fixes — they are over-broad base rules surfaced by the full re-tag (e.g. academic-skills' `learning`/`writing`, subjects-timetable's bare `subject`). **Recommend a Phase II rule-tightening review before these specific counts are quoted.**

| topic | old | new(all) | new(content) | over-tag |
|---|--:|--:|--:|--:|
| placements-WIL | 203 | 230 | 186 | +27 |
| academic-skills | 36 | 47 | 40 | +11 |

**Untagged residual:** 60 content pages match no topic keyword (e.g. "Student Newsletters", "Key dates", committee pages) and are left untagged by design — conservative (under-count) rather than the old over-count. They are excluded from topic counts.
